{"tool": "read_file", "status": "ok", "content": "1: /* Copyright 2018 ETH Zurich and University of Bologna.
2:  * Copyright and related rights are licensed under the Solderpad Hardware
3:  * License, Version 0.51 (the “License”); you may not use this file except in
4:  * compliance with the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
5:  * http://solderpad.org/licenses/SHL-0.51. Unless required by applicable law
6:  * or agreed to in writing, software, hardware and materials distributed under
7:  * this License is distributed on an “AS IS” BASIS, WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR
8:  * CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. See the License for the
9:  * specific language governing permissions and limitations under the License.
10:  *
11:  * File:  dm_csrs.sv
12:  * Author: Florian Zaruba <zarubaf@iis.ee.ethz.ch>
13:  * Date:   30.6.2018
14:  *
15:  * Description: Debug CSRs. Communication over Debug Transport Module (DTM)
16:  */
17: 
18: module dm_csrs #(
19:     parameter int NrHarts = -1
20: ) (
21:     input  logic                              clk_i,              // Clock
22:     input  logic                              rst_ni,             // Asynchronous reset active low
23:     input  logic                              testmode_i,
24:     input  logic                              dmi_rst_ni,         // Debug Module Interface reset, active-low
25:     input  logic                              dmi_req_valid_i,
26:     output logic                              dmi_req_ready_o,
27:     input  dm::dmi_req_t                      dmi_req_i,
28:     // every request needs a response one cycle later
29:     output logic                              dmi_resp_valid_o,
30:     input  logic                              dmi_resp_ready_i,
31:     output dm::dmi_resp_t                     dmi_resp_o,
32:     // global ctrl
33:     output logic                              ndmreset_o,      // non-debug module reset, active-high
34:     output logic                              dmactive_o,      // 1 -> debug-module is active, 0 -> synchronous re-set
35:     // hart status
36:     input  dm::hartinfo_t [NrHarts-1:0]       hartinfo_i,      // static hartinfo
37:     input  logic [NrHarts-1:0]                halted_i,        // hart is halted
38:     input  logic [NrHarts-1:0]                unavailable_i,   // e.g.: powered down
39:     input  logic [NrHarts-1:0]                resumeack_i,     // hart acknowledged resume request
40:     // hart control
41:     output logic [19:0]                       hartsel_o,       // hartselect to ctrl module
42:     output logic [NrHarts-1:0]                haltreq_o,       // request to halt a hart
43:     output logic [NrHarts-1:0]                resumereq_o,     // request hart to resume
44: 
45:     output logic                              cmd_valid_o,       // debugger is writing to the command field
46:     output dm::command_t                      cmd_o,             // abstract command
47:     input  logic                              cmderror_valid_i,  // an error occured
48:     input  dm::cmderr_t                       cmderror_i,        // this error occured
49:     input  logic                              cmdbusy_i,         // cmd is currently busy executing
50: 
51:     output logic [dm::ProgBufSize-1:0][31:0]  progbuf_o, // to system bus
52:     output logic [dm::DataCount-1:0][31:0]    data_o,
53: 
54:     input  logic [dm::DataCount-1:0][31:0]    data_i,
55:     input  logic                              data_valid_i,
56:     // system bus access module (SBA)
57:     output logic [63:0]                       sbaddress_o,
58:     input  logic [63:0]                       sbaddress_i,
59:     output logic                              sbaddress_write_valid_o,
60:     // control signals in
61:     output logic                              sbreadonaddr_o,
62:     output logic                              sbautoincrement_o
[TRUNCATED]

{"tool": "read_file", "status": "ok", "content": "1: // Copyright 2018 ETH Zurich and University of Bologna.
2: // Copyright and related rights are licensed under the Solderpad Hardware
3: // License, Version 0.51 (the "License"); you may not use this file except in
4: // compliance with the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
5: // http://solderpad.org/licenses/SHL-0.51. Unless required by applicable law
6: // or agreed to in writing, software, hardware and materials distributed under
7: // this License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS, WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR
8: // CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. See the License for the
9: // specific language governing permissions and limitations under the License.
10: //
11: // Author: Florian Zaruba, ETH Zurich
12: // Date: 05.05.2017
13: // Description: CSR Register File as specified by RISC-V
14: 
15: import ariane_pkg::*;
16: 
17: module csr_regfile #(
18:     parameter int          ASID_WIDTH      = 1,
19:     parameter int unsigned NR_COMMIT_PORTS = 2
20: ) (
21:     input  logic                  clk_i,                      // Clock
22:     input  logic                  rst_ni,                     // Asynchronous reset active low
23:     input  logic                  time_irq_i,                 // Timer threw a interrupt
24:     // send a flush request out if a CSR with a side effect has changed (e.g. written)
25:     output logic                  flush_o,
26:     output logic                  halt_csr_o,                 // halt requested
27:     // commit acknowledge
28:     input  scoreboard_entry_t [NR_COMMIT_PORTS-1:0] commit_instr_i, // the instruction we want to commit
29:     input  logic [NR_COMMIT_PORTS-1:0]              commit_ack_i,   // Commit acknowledged a instruction -> increase instret CSR
30:     // Core and Cluster ID
31:     input  logic  [63:0]          boot_addr_i,                // Address from which to start booting, mtvec is set to the same address
32:     input  logic  [63:0]          hart_id_i,                  // Hart id in a multicore environment (reflected in a CSR)
33:     // we are taking an exception
34:     input exception_t             ex_i,                       // We've got an exception from the commit stage, take its
35: 
36:     input  fu_op                  csr_op_i,                   // Operation to perform on the CSR file
37:     input  logic  [11:0]          csr_addr_i,                 // Address of the register to read/write
38:     input  logic  [63:0]          csr_wdata_i,                // Write data in
39:     output logic  [63:0]          csr_rdata_o,                // Read data out
40:     input  logic                  dirty_fp_state_i,           // Mark the FP sate as dirty
41:     input  logic                  csr_write_fflags_i,         // Write fflags register e.g.: we are retiring a floating point instruction
42:     input  logic  [63:0]          pc_i,                       // PC of instruction accessing the CSR
43:     output exception_t            csr_exception_o,            // attempts to access a CSR without appropriate privilege
44:                                                               // level or to write  a read-only register also
45:                                                               // raises illegal instruction exceptions.
46:     // Interrupts/Exceptions
47:     output logic  [63:0]          epc_o,                      // Output the exception PC to PC Gen, the correct CSR (mepc, sepc) is set accordingly
48:     output logic                  eret_o,                     // Return from exception, set the PC of epc_o
49:     output logic  [63:0]          trap_vector_base_o,         // Output base of exception vector, correct CSR is output (mtvec, stvec)
50:     output riscv::priv_lvl_t      priv_lvl_o,                 // Current privilege level the CPU is in
51:     // FPU
52:     output riscv::xs_t            fs_o,   
[TRUNCATED]

200:             end
201:         end
202: 
203:         if (shift_dr) begin
204:             if (dmi_access) dr_d = {dmi_tdi, dr_q[$bits(dr_q)-1:1]};
205:         end
206: 
207:         if (test_logic_reset) begin
208:             dr_d = '0;
209:         end
210:     end
211: 
212:     always_ff @(posedge tck_i or negedge trst_ni) begin
213:         if (~trst_ni) begin
214:             dr_q      <= '0;
215:             state_q   <= Idle;
216:             address_q <= '0;
217:             data_q    <= '0;
218:             error_q   <= DMINoError;
219:         end else begin
220:             dr_q      <= dr_d;
221:             state_q   <= state_d;
222:             address_q <= address_d;
223:             data_q    <= data_d;
224:             error_q   <= error_d;
225:         end
226:     end
227:     
228: // On reset, key value input jtag_key from rom2 copied to register
229:     always_ff @ (posedge clk_i) begin
230:         if (~rst_ni) begin
231:             pass <= jtag_key;
232:         end 
233:     end
234:  
235:     // ---------
236:     // TAP
237:     // ---------
238:     dmi_jtag_tap #(
239:         .IrLength (5)
240:     ) i_dmi_jtag_tap (
241:         .tck_i,
242:         .tms_i,
243:         .trst_ni,
244:         .td_i,
245:         .td_o,
246:         .tdo_oe_o,
247:         .testmode_i         ( testmode_i       ),
248:         .test_logic_reset_o ( test_logic_reset ),
249:         .shift_dr_o         ( shift_dr         ),
250:         .update_dr_o        ( update_dr        ),
251:         .capture_dr_o       ( capture_dr       ),
252:         .dmi_access_o       ( dmi_access       ),
253:         .dtmcs_select_o     ( dtmcs_select     ),
254:         .dmi_reset_o        ( dmi_reset        ),
255:         .dmi_error_i        ( error_q          ),
256:         .dmi_tdi_o          ( dmi_tdi          ),
257:         .dmi_tdo_i          ( dmi_tdo          )
258:     );
259: 
260:     // ---------
261:     // CDC
262:     // ---------
263:     dmi_cdc i_dmi_cdc (
264:         // JTAG side (master side)
265:         .tck_i,
266:         .trst_ni,
267:         .jtag_dmi_req_i    ( dmi_req          ),
268:         .jtag_dmi_ready_o  ( dmi_req_ready    ),
269:         .jtag_dmi_valid_i  ( dmi_req_valid    ),
270:         .jtag_dmi_resp_o   ( dmi_resp         ),
271:         .jtag_dmi_valid_o  ( dmi_resp_valid   ),
272:         .jtag_dmi_ready_i  ( dmi_resp_ready   ),
273:         // core side
274:         .clk_i,
275:         .rst_ni,
276:         .core_dmi_req_o    ( dmi_req_o        ),
277:         .core_dmi_valid_o  ( dmi_req_valid_o  ),
278:         .core_dmi_ready_i  ( dmi_req_ready_i  ),
279:         .core_dmi_resp_i   ( dmi_resp_i       ),
280:         .core_dmi_ready_o  ( dmi_resp_ready_o ),
281:         .core_dmi_valid_i  ( dmi_resp_valid_i )
282:     );
283: 
284: endmodule

296:         // write
297:         if (dmi_req_ready_o && dmi_req_valid_i && dtm_op == dm::DTM_WRITE) begin
298:             unique case (dm::dm_csr_t'({1'b0, dmi_req_i.addr})) inside
299:                 [(dm::Data0):DataEnd]: begin
300:                     // attempts to write them while busy is set does not change their value
301:                     if (!cmdbusy_i && dm::DataCount > 0) begin
302:                         data_d[dmi_req_i.addr[4:0]] = dmi_req_i.data;
303:                         // check whether we need to re-execute the command (just give a cmd_valid)
304:                         cmd_valid_o = abstractauto_q.autoexecdata[dmi_req_i.addr[3:0] - int'(dm::Data0)];
305:                     end
306:                 end
307:                 dm::DMControl: begin
308:                     automatic dm::dmcontrol_t dmcontrol;
309:                     dmcontrol = dm::dmcontrol_t'(dmi_req_i.data);
310:                     // clear the havreset of the selected hart
311:                     if (dmcontrol.ackhavereset) begin
312:                         havereset_d[selected_hart] = 1'b0;
313:                     end
314:                     dmcontrol_d = dmi_req_i.data;
315:                 end
316:                 dm::DMStatus:; // write are ignored to R/O register
317:                 dm::Hartinfo:; // hartinfo is R/O
318:                 // only command error is write-able
319:                 dm::AbstractCS: begin // W1C
320:                     // Gets set if an abstract command fails. The bits in this
321:                     // field remain set until they are cleared by writing 1 to
322:                     // them. No abstract command is started until the value is
323:                     // reset to 0.
324:                     automatic dm::abstractcs_t a_abstractcs;
325:                     a_abstractcs = dm::abstractcs_t'(dmi_req_i.data);
326:                     // reads during abstract command execution are not allowed
327:                     if (!cmdbusy_i) begin
328:                         cmderr_d = dm::cmderr_t'(~a_abstractcs.cmderr & cmderr_q);
329:                     end else if (cmderr_q == dm::CmdErrNone) begin
330:                         cmderr_d = dm::CmdErrBusy;
331:                     end
332: 
333:                 end
334:                 dm::Command: begin
335:                     // writes are ignored if a command is already busy
336:                     if (!cmdbusy_i) begin
337:                         cmd_valid_o = 1'b1;
338:                         command_d = dm::command_t'(dmi_req_i.data);
339:                     // if there was an attempted to write during a busy execution
340:                     // and the cmderror field is zero set the busy error
341:                     end else if (cmderr_q == dm::CmdErrNone) begin
342:                         cmderr_d = dm::CmdErrBusy;
343:                     end
344:                 end
345:                 dm::AbstractAuto: begin
346:                     // this field can only be written legally when there is no command executing
347:                     if (!cmdbusy_i) begin
348:                         abstractauto_d = {dmi_req_i.data[31:16], 4'b0, dmi_req_i.data[11:0]};
349:                     end else if (cmderr_q == dm::CmdErrNone) begin
350:                         cmderr_d = dm::CmdErrBusy;
351:                     end
352:                 end
353:                 [(dm::ProgBuf0):ProgBufEnd]: begin
354:                     // attempts to write them while busy is set does not change their value
355:                     if (!cmdbusy_i) begin
356:                         progbuf_d[dmi_req_i.addr[4:0]] = dmi_req_i.data;
357:                         // check whether we need to re-execute the command (just give a cmd_valid)
358:                         // this should probably throw an error if executed during another command was busy
359:                         // TODO(zarubaf): check if offset is correct - without it this may assign Xes
[TRUNCATED]

{"tool": "read_file", "status": "ok", "content": "1: /* Copyright 2018 ETH Zurich and University of Bologna.
2:  * Copyright and related rights are licensed under the Solderpad Hardware
3:  * License, Version 0.51 (the “License”); you may not use this file except in
4:  * compliance with the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
5:  * http://solderpad.org/licenses/SHL-0.51. Unless required by applicable law
6:  * or agreed to in writing, software, hardware and materials distributed under
7:  * this License is distributed on an “AS IS” BASIS, WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR
8:  * CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied. See the License for the
9:  * specific language governing permissions and limitations under the License.
10:  *
11:  * File:   dm_top.sv
12:  * Author: Florian Zaruba <zarubaf@iis.ee.ethz.ch>
13:  * Date:   30.6.2018
14:  *
15:  * Description: Top-level of debug module (DM). This is an AXI-Slave.
16:  *              DTM protocol is equal to SiFives debug protocol to leverage
17:  *              SW infrastructure re-use. As of version 0.13
18:  */
19: 
20: module dm_top #(
21:     parameter int NrHarts      = -1,
22:     parameter int AxiIdWidth   = -1,
23:     parameter int AxiAddrWidth = -1,
24:     parameter int AxiDataWidth = -1,
25:     parameter int AxiUserWidth = -1
26: ) (
27:     input  logic               clk_i,       // clock
28:     input  logic               rst_ni,      // asynchronous reset active low, connect PoR here, not the system reset
29:     input  logic               testmode_i,
30:     output logic               ndmreset_o,  // non-debug module reset
31:     output logic               dmactive_o,  // debug module is active
32:     output logic [NrHarts-1:0] debug_req_o, // async debug request
33:     input  logic [NrHarts-1:0] unavailable_i, // communicate whether the hart is unavailable (e.g.: power down)
34: 
35:     // bus slave, for an execution based technique
36:     input  ariane_axi::req_t   axi_s_req_i,
37:     output ariane_axi::resp_t  axi_s_resp_o,
38: 
39:     // bus master, for system bus accesses
40:     output ariane_axi::req_t   axi_m_req_o,
41:     input  ariane_axi::resp_t  axi_m_resp_i,
42: 
43:     // Connection to DTM - compatible to RocketChip Debug Module
44:     input  logic               dmi_rst_ni,
45:     input  logic               dmi_req_valid_i,
46:     output logic               dmi_req_ready_o,
47:     input  dm::dmi_req_t       dmi_req_i,
48: 
49:     output logic               dmi_resp_valid_o,
50:     input  logic               dmi_resp_ready_i,
51:     output dm::dmi_resp_t      dmi_resp_o
52: );
53: 
54:     // Debug CSRs
55:     dm::hartinfo_t [NrHarts-1:0]      hartinfo;
56:     logic [NrHarts-1:0]               halted;
57:     // logic [NrHarts-1:0]               running;
58:     logic [NrHarts-1:0]               resumeack;
59:     logic [NrHarts-1:0]               haltreq;
60:     logic [NrHarts-1:0]               resumereq;
61:     logic                             cmd_valid;
62:     dm::command_t                     cmd;
63: 
64:     logic                             req;
65:     logic                             we;
66:     logic [63:0]                      addr;
67:     logic [7:0]                       be;
68:     logic [63:0]                      wdata;
69:     logic [63:0]                      rdata;
70: 
71:     logic                             cmderror_valid;
72:     dm::cmderr_t                      cmderror;
73:     logic                             cmdbusy;
74:     logic [dm::ProgBufSize-1:0][31:0] progbuf;
75:     logic [dm::DataCount-1:0][31:0]   data_csrs_mem;
76:     logic [dm::DataCount-1:0][31:0]   data_mem_csrs;
77:     logic                             data_valid;
78:     logic [19:0]                      hartsel;
79:     // System Bus Access Module
80:     logic [63:0]                      sbaddress_csrs_sba;
81:     logic [63:0]       
[TRUNCATED]

120:     // Converts to DPI calls
121:     SimJTAG i_SimJTAG (
122:         .clock                ( clk_i                ),
123:         .reset                ( ~rst_ni              ),
124:         .enable               ( jtag_enable[0]       ),
125:         .init_done            ( init_done            ),
126:         .jtag_TCK             ( jtag_TCK             ),
127:         .jtag_TMS             ( jtag_TMS             ),
128:         .jtag_TDI             ( jtag_TDI             ),
129:         .jtag_TRSTn           ( jtag_TRSTn           ),
130:         .jtag_TDO_data        ( jtag_TDO_data        ),
131:         .jtag_TDO_driven      ( jtag_TDO_driven      ),
132:         .exit                 ( jtag_exit            )
133:     );
134: 
135:     dmi_jtag i_dmi_jtag (
136:         .clk_i            ( clk_i           ),
137:         .rst_ni           ( rst_ni          ),
138:         .testmode_i       ( test_en         ),
139:         .jtag_key         ( jtag_key        ), 
140:         .dmi_req_o        ( jtag_dmi_req    ),
141:         .dmi_req_valid_o  ( jtag_req_valid  ),
142:         .dmi_req_ready_i  ( debug_req_ready ),
143:         .dmi_resp_i       ( debug_resp      ),
144:         .dmi_resp_ready_o ( jtag_resp_ready ),
145:         .dmi_resp_valid_i ( jtag_resp_valid ),
146:         .dmi_rst_no       (                 ), // not connected
147:         .tck_i            ( jtag_TCK        ),
148:         .tms_i            ( jtag_TMS        ),
149:         .trst_ni          ( jtag_TRSTn      ),
150:         .td_i             ( jtag_TDI        ),
151:         .td_o             ( jtag_TDO_data   ),
152:         .tdo_oe_o         ( jtag_TDO_driven ),
153: 	.umode_o          ( ariane_umode    ) 
154:     );
155: 
156:     // SiFive's SimDTM Module
157:     // Converts to DPI calls
158:     logic [1:0] debug_req_bits_op;
159:     assign dmi_req.op = dm::dtm_op_t'(debug_req_bits_op);
160: 
161:     if (InclSimDTM) begin
162:         SimDTM i_SimDTM (
163:             .clk                  ( clk_i                ),
164:             .reset                ( ~rst_ni              ),
165:             .debug_req_valid      ( dmi_req_valid        ),
166:             .debug_req_ready      ( debug_req_ready      ),
167:             .debug_req_bits_addr  ( dmi_req.addr         ),
168:             .debug_req_bits_op    ( debug_req_bits_op    ),
169:             .debug_req_bits_data  ( dmi_req.data         ),
170:             .debug_resp_valid     ( dmi_resp_valid       ),
171:             .debug_resp_ready     ( dmi_resp_ready       ),
172:             .debug_resp_bits_resp ( debug_resp.resp      ),
173:             .debug_resp_bits_data ( debug_resp.data      ),
174:             .exit                 ( dmi_exit             )
175:         );
176:     end else begin
177:         assign dmi_req_valid = '0;
178:         assign debug_req_bits_op = '0;
179:         assign dmi_exit = 1'b0;
180:     end
181: 
182:     ariane_axi::req_t    dm_axi_m_req,  dm_axi_s_req;
183:     ariane_axi::resp_t   dm_axi_m_resp, dm_axi_s_resp;
184: 
185:     // debug module
186:     dm_top #(
187:         // current implementation only supports 1 hart
188:         .NrHarts              ( 1                         ),
189:         .AxiIdWidth           ( AXI_ID_WIDTH_SLAVES       ),
190:         .AxiAddrWidth         ( AXI_ADDRESS_WIDTH         ),
191:         .AxiDataWidth         ( AXI_DATA_WIDTH            ),
192:         .AxiUserWidth         ( AXI_USER_WIDTH            )
193:     ) i_dm_top (
194: 
195:         .clk_i                ( clk_i                ),
196:         .rst_ni               ( rst_ni               ), // PoR
197:         .testmode_i           ( test_en              ),
198:         .ndmreset_o           ( ndmreset             ),
199:         .dmactive_o           (                      ), // active debug session
200:         .debug_req_o          ( debug_req_core       ),
201:         .unavailable_i        ( '0                   ),
202:      
[TRUNCATED]

150:     dm_sba i_dm_sba (
151:         .clk_i                   ( clk_i                 ),
152:         .rst_ni                  ( rst_ni                ),
153:         .dmactive_i              ( dmactive_o            ),
154:         .axi_req_o               ( axi_m_req_o           ),
155:         .axi_resp_i              ( axi_m_resp_i          ),
156:         .sbaddress_i             ( sbaddress_csrs_sba    ),
157:         .sbaddress_o             ( sbaddress_sba_csrs    ),
158:         .sbaddress_write_valid_i ( sbaddress_write_valid ),
159:         .sbreadonaddr_i          ( sbreadonaddr          ),
160:         .sbautoincrement_i       ( sbautoincrement       ),
161:         .sbaccess_i              ( sbaccess              ),
162:         .sbreadondata_i          ( sbreadondata          ),
163:         .sbdata_i                ( sbdata_write          ),
164:         .sbdata_read_valid_i     ( sbdata_read_valid     ),
165:         .sbdata_write_valid_i    ( sbdata_write_valid    ),
166:         .sbdata_o                ( sbdata_read           ),
167:         .sbdata_valid_o          ( sbdata_valid          ),
168:         .sbbusy_o                ( sbbusy                ),
169:         .sberror_valid_o         ( sberror_valid         ),
170:         .sberror_o               ( sberror               )
171:     );
172: 
173:     dm_mem #(
174:         .NrHarts (NrHarts)
175:     ) i_dm_mem (
176:         .clk_i                   ( clk_i                 ),
177:         .rst_ni                  ( rst_ni                ),
178:         .debug_req_o             ( debug_req_o           ),
179:         .hartsel_i               ( hartsel               ),
180:         .haltreq_i               ( haltreq               ),
181:         .resumereq_i             ( resumereq             ),
182:         .halted_o                ( halted                ),
183:         .resuming_o              ( resumeack             ),
184:         .cmd_valid_i             ( cmd_valid             ),
185:         .cmd_i                   ( cmd                   ),
186:         .cmderror_valid_o        ( cmderror_valid        ),
187:         .cmderror_o              ( cmderror              ),
188:         .cmdbusy_o               ( cmdbusy               ),
189:         .progbuf_i               ( progbuf               ),
190:         .data_i                  ( data_csrs_mem         ),
191:         .data_o                  ( data_mem_csrs         ),
192:         .data_valid_o            ( data_valid            ),
193:         .req_i                   ( req                   ),
194:         .we_i                    ( we                    ),
195:         .addr_i                  ( addr                  ),
196:         .wdata_i                 ( wdata                 ),

{"tool": "read_file", "status": "ok", "content": "600:             priv_lvl_d = trap_to_priv_lvl;
601:         end
602: 
603:         // ------------------------------
604:         // Debug
605:         // ------------------------------
606:         // Explains why Debug Mode was entered.
607:         // When there are multiple reasons to enter Debug Mode in a single cycle, hardware should set cause to the cause with the highest priority.
608:         // 1: An ebreak instruction was executed. (priority 3)
609:         // 2: The Trigger Module caused a breakpoint exception. (priority 4)
610:         // 3: The debugger requested entry to Debug Mode. (priority 2)
611:         // 4: The hart single stepped because step was set. (priority 1)
612:         // we are currently not in debug mode and could potentially enter
613:         if (!debug_mode_q) begin
614:             dcsr_d.prv = priv_lvl_o;
615:             // trigger module fired
616: 
617:             // caused by a breakpoint
618:             if (ex_i.valid && ex_i.cause == riscv::BREAKPOINT) begin
619:                 // check that we actually want to enter debug depending on the privilege level we are currently in
620:                 unique case (priv_lvl_o)
621:                     riscv::PRIV_LVL_M: begin
622:                         debug_mode_d = dcsr_q.ebreakm;
623:                         set_debug_pc_o = dcsr_q.ebreakm;
624:                     end
625:                     riscv::PRIV_LVL_S: begin
626:                         debug_mode_d = dcsr_q.ebreaks;
627:                         set_debug_pc_o = dcsr_q.ebreaks;
628:                     end
629:                     riscv::PRIV_LVL_U: begin
630:                         debug_mode_d = dcsr_q.ebreaku;
631:                         set_debug_pc_o = dcsr_q.ebreaku;
632:                     end
633:                     default:;
634:                 endcase
635:                 // save PC of next this instruction e.g.: the next one to be executed
636:                 dpc_d = pc_i;
637:                 dcsr_d.cause = dm::CauseBreakpoint;
638:             end
639: 
640:             // we've got a debug request (and we have an instruction which we can associate it to)
641:             // don't interrupt the AMO
642:             if (debug_req_i && commit_instr_i[0].valid) begin
643:                 // save the PC
644:                 dpc_d = pc_i;
645:                 // enter debug mode
646:                 debug_mode_d = 1'b1;
647:                 // jump to the base address
648:                 set_debug_pc_o = 1'b1;
649:                 // save the cause as external debug request
650:                 dcsr_d.cause = dm::CauseRequest;
651:             end
652: 
653:             // single step enable and we just retired an instruction
654:             if (dcsr_q.step && commit_ack_i[0]) begin
655:                 // valid CTRL flow change
656:                 if (commit_instr_i[0].fu == CTRL_FLOW) begin
657:                     // we saved the correct target address during execute
658:                     dpc_d = commit_instr_i[0].bp.predict_address;
659:                 // exception valid
660:                 end else if (ex_i.valid) begin
661:                     dpc_d = trap_vector_base_o;
662:                 // return from environment
663:                 end else if (eret_o) begin
664:                     dpc_d = epc_o;
665:                 // consecutive PC
666:                 end else begin
667:                     dpc_d = commit_instr_i[0].pc + (commit_instr_i[0].is_compressed ? 'h2 : 'h4);
668:                 end
669:                 debug_mode_d = 1'b1;
670:                 set_debug_pc_o = 1'b1;
671:                 dcsr_d.cause = dm::CauseSingleStep;
672:             end
673:         end
674:         // go in halt-state again when we encounter an exception
675:         if (debug_mode_q && ex_i.valid && ex_i.cause == riscv::BREAK
[TRUNCATED]

468:                 end
469:                 // mask the register so that unsupported interrupts can never be set
470:                 riscv::CSR_MIE: begin
471:                     mask = riscv::MIP_SSIP | riscv::MIP_STIP | riscv::MIP_SEIP | riscv::MIP_MSIP | riscv::MIP_MTIP;
472:                     mie_d = (mie_q & ~mask) | (csr_wdata & mask); // we only support supervisor and M-mode interrupts
473:                 end
474: 
475:                 riscv::CSR_MTVEC: begin
476:                     mtvec_d = {csr_wdata[63:2], 1'b0, csr_wdata[0]};
477:                     // we are in vector mode, this implementation requires the additional
478:                     // alignment constraint of 64 * 4 bytes
479:                     if (csr_wdata[0]) mtvec_d = {csr_wdata[63:8], 7'b0, csr_wdata[0]};
480:                 end
481:                 riscv::CSR_MCOUNTEREN:;
482: 
483:                 riscv::CSR_MSCRATCH:           mscratch_d  = csr_wdata;
484:                 riscv::CSR_MEPC:               mepc_d      = {csr_wdata[63:1], 1'b0};
485:                 riscv::CSR_MCAUSE:             mcause_d    = csr_wdata;
486:                 riscv::CSR_MTVAL:              mtval_d     = csr_wdata;
487:                 riscv::CSR_MIP: begin
488:                     mask = riscv::MIP_SSIP | riscv::MIP_STIP | riscv::MIP_SEIP;
489:                     mip_d = (mip_q & ~mask) | (csr_wdata & mask);
490:                 end
491:                 // performance counters
492:                 riscv::CSR_MCYCLE:             cycle_d     = csr_wdata;
493:                 riscv::CSR_MINSTRET:           instret     = csr_wdata;
494:                 riscv::CSR_DCACHE:             dcache_d    = csr_wdata[0]; // enable bit
495:                 riscv::CSR_ICACHE:             icache_d    = csr_wdata[0]; // enable bit
496:                 riscv::CSR_L1_ICACHE_MISS,
497:                 riscv::CSR_L1_DCACHE_MISS,
498:                 riscv::CSR_ITLB_MISS,
499:                 riscv::CSR_DTLB_MISS,
500:                 riscv::CSR_LOAD,
501:                 riscv::CSR_STORE,
502:                 riscv::CSR_EXCEPTION,
503:                 riscv::CSR_EXCEPTION_RET,
504:                 riscv::CSR_BRANCH_JUMP,
505:                 riscv::CSR_CALL,
506:                 riscv::CSR_RET,
507:                 riscv::CSR_MIS_PREDICT: begin
508:                                         perf_data_o = csr_wdata;
509:                                         perf_we_o   = 1'b1;
510:                 end
511:                 default: update_access_exception = 1'b1;
512:             endcase
513:         end
514:

516:         mstatus_d.uxl  = riscv::XLEN_64;
517: 
518:         // mark the floating point extension register as dirty
519:         if (FP_PRESENT && (dirty_fp_state_csr || dirty_fp_state_i)) begin
520:             mstatus_d.fs = riscv::Dirty;
521:         end
522: 
523:         // write the floating point status register

525:             fcsr_d.fflags = csr_wdata_i[4:0] | fcsr_q.fflags;

527:         // ---------------------

531:         mip_d[riscv::IRQ_M_EXT] = irq_i[0];
532:         // Machine software interrupt

534:         // Timer interrupt pending, coming from platform timer
535:         mip_d[riscv::IRQ_M_TIMER] = time_irq_i;

1:  /*
2:  * ROM2: Which have all the keys.
3:  */
4: 
5: module rom2 (
6:    input  logic         clk_i,
7:    input  logic         rst_ni,
8:    input  logic         req_i,
9:    input  logic         we_i,
10:    input  logic [63:0]  addr_i,
11:    input  logic [63:0]  wdata_i,
12:    output logic [63:0]  rdata_o,
13:    output logic [3:0][191:0]  secure_reg // Ideally [1:0] should be [RomSize-1:0]. In this case RomSize is 2.
14: );
15:     localparam int RomSize = 4;
16: 
17: // Store key values here. // Replication of fuse. 
18:     const logic [RomSize-1:0][191:0] mem = {
19:         192'h00000000_00000000_00000000_00000000_0000f8f8_ff6fe00f, // 3rd location for Access control master 1. First 4 bits for peripheral 0, next 4 for p1 and so on.
20:         192'h00000000_00000000_00000000_00000000_0000fff8_ff6ff00f, // 2nd location for Access control master 0. First 4 bits for peripheral 0, next 4 for p1 and so on.
21:         192'h2b7e1516_28aed2a6_abf71588_09cf4f3c_2b7e1516_28aed2a6, // 1st location for JTAG
22:         192'h55555555_28aed2a6_abf71588_09cf4f3c_2b7e1516_28aed2a6  // oth location for AES
23:     };
24: 
25: // Secure registers. Key values copied from fuse to these registers on reset.
26: //    logic [RomSize-1:0][191:0] secure_reg;
27: 
28:     logic [$clog2(RomSize)-1:0] raddr_q;
29: 
30: // We can read and write initial 64 bits.
31: // On reset, key values will be copied to registers.
32:     always_ff @ (posedge clk_i) begin
33:         if (~rst_ni) begin
34:             secure_reg <= mem;
35:         end else begin
36:         if(req_i) begin
37:             if (!we_i) begin
38:                 raddr_q <= addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3];
39:             end else begin
40:                 secure_reg[addr_i[$clog2(RomSize)-1+3:3]] <= wdata_i;
41:             end
42:         end
43:         end
44:     end
45:     // this prevents spurious Xes from propagating into
46:     // the speculative fetch stage of the core
47:     assign rdata_o = (raddr_q < RomSize) ? secure_reg[raddr_q] : '0;
48: 
49: 
50: endmodule

      "obligation_id": "PERM-DBG-JTAG-AUTH-001",
      "status": "candidate_violation",
      "subject": "External JTAG/debug requester through SimJTAG/TAP",
      "operation": "read/write DMI registers and shift DMI responses out over TDO",
      "object": "DMI request/response path into dm_top/dm_csrs, including debug CSRs and SBA controls",
      "expected_guard": "DMI requests and returned response data should be gated by JTAG authentication such as jtag_key/pass_chk before dmi_req_valid_o can issue and before response data is shifted out.",
      "observed_behavior": "Authentication exists but is incomplete. In dmi_jtag, DTM_READ is gated by pass_chk, but DTM_WRITE transitions to Write without checking pass_chk. dmi_req_valid is asserted in Write and Read states. Response data is captured into data_d on dmi_resp_valid and shifted through dmi_tdo/dr_q when DMIACCESS is selected, without pass_chk gating. dm_csrs also reports dmstatus.authenticated = 1'b1 unconditionally and has no pass_chk input.",
      "path": "td_i/tck/tms -> dmi_jtag_tap DMIACCESS -> dmi_jtag dr_q/state_q -> dmi_cdc -> dm_top -> dm_csrs; response dm_csrs -> dmi_cdc -> dmi_jtag data_q/dr_q -> dmi_tdo -> dmi_jtag_tap td_o",
      "state_condition": "pass_chk false/unauthenticated; dmi_access asserted by TAP IR DMIACCESS; update_dr with DTM_WRITE still enters Write; dmi_req_valid asserted; capture_dr/shift_dr can expose data_q over TDO.",
      "impact": "An unauthenticated JTAG requester can issue DMI writes and can potentially receive/shift stale or returned DMI data, enabling debug CSR/SBA control despite an apparent key check.",
      "rtl_evidence": [
        "src/debug/dmi_jtag.sv:106-114: Idle state accepts dmi_access/update_dr; read checks pass_chk, write does not.",
        "src/debug/dmi_jtag.sv:125-146: Read and Write states assert dmi_req_valid.",
        "src/debug/dmi_jtag.sv:132-136: dmi_resp_valid loads dmi_resp.data into data_d.",
        "src/debug/dmi_jtag.sv:187-205: dmi_tdo = dr_q[0]; shift_dr shifts DMI register when dmi_access, no pass_chk condition.",
        "src/debug/dmi_jtag.sv:263-281: dmi_cdc forwards jtag_dmi_valid_i to core_dmi_valid_o.",
        "src/debug/dmi_jtag_tap.sv:175-185 and 201-205: DMIACCESS selects dmi_access_o and dmi_tdo_i to TDO.",
        "src/debug/dm_csrs.sv:168-172: dmstatus.authenticated hardwired to 1'b1."
